subject: fix: add /embed path route for cross-origin iframe support
body:
The X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN header (audit fix H4, 19e1e21)
blocked cross-origin iframes, breaking embeds in see-veo and other
apps. Vercel matches headers by path only, not query params, so
the /?embed= route couldn't be exempted.

Solution: Add /embed path with X-Frame-Options exempted in vercel.json.
New URL format: /embed?charts=id1,id2 (cross-origin safe).
Legacy /?embed= still works for same-origin use.

Changes:
- vercel.json: restore SAMEORIGIN, exempt /embed and /embed.js paths
- App.jsx: detect embed mode from /embed path + ?charts= param
- index.html: skip debug pill on /embed path
- EMBED_REFERENCE.md: document new /embed URL format

Note: see-veo needs to update EMBED_BASE or iframe URLs to use
/embed?charts= instead of /?embed= for cross-origin to work.

Tags: security, embed, bugfix
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 4
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: embed-system
Semver: patch

title: add /embed path route for cross-origin iframe support
